entry.title: The correct order to change SDCCN source flag destinations to point to the new solution Manager
entry.content:
We have reinstalled a new solution manager system, now with the SID of PSM. Our previous on-prem solution manager is now disabled. Managed system setup has been done for our S4 systems, using the new AWS solman, PSM. I am now trying to change managed systems' SDCCN source flag to the new solution Manager. I can not determine the proper way to do that. 

Managed system setup has been done for our S4 systems.  I am now trying to change SDCCN source flag destinations to point to the new solution Manager.   I can not determine the proper way to do that.  I reviewed these:

but they were not specific to my issue.

In our SD1 S4 system, SDCCN – Goto settings --> Task specific, the old PSO solution manager system is still listed as the “source”.  I would like to follow the proper sequence to remove the old PSO solution manager, to the new PSM solution manager in sdccn of all of our managed systems. 

 

Please advise.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The correct order to change SDCCN source flag destina...
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

Greetings from SAP Product Support.

I would like to recommend the following order to set the source flag to your new solman in SD1 SDCCN:

1. Delete the old BACK RFC pointing to the old PSO solution manager system that listed as the “source” in the managed system's SDCCN.
2. Delete the old BACK RFC that points to the old PSO system in the managed system in SM59.
3. Ensure the Managed System Configuration setup steps are finished without any error in your new solman.
4. Add the New BACK RFC destination manually in SDCCN in your managed system and set the "Source" flag via SDCCN->Goto -> Settings -> Task specific -> RFC destinations -> Settings -> Destinations)

Hope the above helps to clarify.

Sincerely,
Yuki
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Story/report to show all members of a Public Dimension
entry.content: I was able to get this to work. I was adding the properties from the add dimension option itself as opposed to specifically adding them from the properties option of the dimension. When I add properties it works fine.

How did you add the directory service of that?

entry.title: How to execute SI-CHECK when there are multiple production clients?
entry.content:
We are planning to migrate our ECC system with multiple production clients to an S/4HANA system with a brownfield conversion.
I have reviewed materials such as SAP Note 2270339 , but I couldn't find specific guidance on executing the SI-CHECK process for multiple production clients.
Regarding the SI-CHECK process, I would like to clarify the following:
・What steps are necessary for each client?
・Are there any steps that can be omitted for specific clients?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to execute SI-CHECK when there are multiple produ...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

According to Note 2399707 question "6.6. In which client should I perform the simplification item checks?" and SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item Check - How to do ... - SAP Community, it needs to execute the Relevance Check on each of the productive clients, then execute the Relevance Check & Consistency Check on the client 000 as it is the same client that SUM will run during the technical conversion. Only running the checks in client 000 will ensure, that data in all clients is checked for inconsistencies. If you run the check in any other client, some of the checks will only return check results for this specific client. Please refer to the following steps:

On all productive clients:

  1. Start report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK in transaction SA38.
  2. In the Simplification Item Check Options section, choose the target SAP S/4HANA product version.
  3. Choose New relevance check in Online mode
  4. Execute the check to receive the simplification item list.

On client 000:

  1. Start report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK in transaction SA38.
  2. In the Simplification Item Check Options section, choose the target SAP S/4HANA product version.
  3. Choose New relevance & consistency check as background job 
  4. Execute the check to receive the simplification item list.
  5. Review the results and resolve all inconsistency. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: 请问怎样修改S-user账号的密码？
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To reset S-user individual password, kindly refer to KBA 1808560 and reset the password via the URL https://accounts.sap.com/ui/createForgottenPasswordMail?spId=55365985e4b07dc3abdfc16c&targetUrl=&sourceUrl=.

Thanks and best regards,
Joanna Zhao
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Unable to send the IDOC from SAP to PI system
entry.content:
Thanks Avinash for posting this detailed response. I embraced point 3 as I was trying to re use RFC Destination and the ProgramID for IDoc_AAE which was prviously created for RFC Adapter Sender channel and ended with same issue.

Those who encounter this very issue, take note that IDoc Sender Communication Channel will not receive anything and will not even show error on Ping Test. However, when you get into RFC Sender Channel, errors will be reflected and it will be similar to what you will see in SM58.

entry.title: Re: AGGREGATION ON CDS VIEW and Filtering data with where clause issue
entry.content:
Thanks for reply.

Tried already.

If CDS views run independently the result is as follows.

CDS1 has 4 entries having different werks and lgort. It is as expected.

CDS2 make it one entry by summing  LABST for CDS1 4 entries. It looks fine.

 program also gives one entry which is fine but it is same as CDS2 ( which has SUM of all weeks, lgort).. is not expected because in where clause I passed hard-coded value to CDS1 fields same as it is in database. I don't think conversion issue here. It should have first filtered based on where clause of CDS1 and then SUM through CDS2.

entry.title: Re: Multiple bank in bank selection (FPY1 Payment run) depending the contract account
entry.content: Hi Amlan, thanks a lot, it's working :

entry.title: Re: 公司间销售业务的按单生产业务
entry.content: Hi Feng_chen，感谢你的回复。我看到样例是使用1HO，但是，在跨国贸易中这个功能不适用。在跨国贸易中，集团公司是贸易公司A和制造公司B。而贸易公司A与外部客户的确认时间点是以客户签收单，而贸易公司A与制造公司B是以FOB离港日作为确认成本和收入，这两个日期是不一致，且时间间隔会跨月。而1HO中，有两个问题：1）必须先开了对外部客户的客户发票后，才能开集团内部发票，这是时间上是有跨月差异；2）销售发货时，贸易公司A与制造公司B同时确认了成本，这也是在时间上存在差异。所以，面对这种跨国贸易的情况，我才想使用5D2来应对，但是，突然发现，制造公司B不支持Make-to-order 或WBS。

entry.title: Re: SAP Build process automation - Document Template - Error
entry.content:
Hi,

We created the subaccount again and the probelm was resolved

entry.title: Remediation step on weak TLS vulnerability
entry.content:
I need on how to remediate system vulnerability reported by our VA tool. It is related to TLS. The step suggested by the tool is very general and not specific for SAP ECC on Suse Linux environment. Please find the attached file for detailed vulnerability.

 

Regards,

 

Zaharah


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Remediation step on weak TLS vulnerability
entry.content:
I can provide a general approach to addressing TLS vulnerabilities in such an environment. Here are the steps you can follow:

  1. Review the Vulnerability Report: Carefully read the vulnerability report generated by your VA tool. Identify the specific TLS-related issues, such as weak ciphers, outdated protocols, or misconfigurations.

  2. Update OpenSSL: Ensure that OpenSSL is up-to-date on your SUSE Linux system. You can update OpenSSL using the following commands:

    sudo zypper refresh
sudo zypper update openssl

  3. Configure Strong Ciphers and Protocols:

    • Edit the OpenSSL configuration file, typically located at /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf or /etc/ssl/openssl.conf.
    • Ensure that only strong ciphers and protocols are enabled. For example, you can disable SSLv2 and SSLv3 and enable TLSv1.2 and TLSv1.3:
      [system_default_sect]
MinProtocol = TLSv1.2
CipherString = DEFAULT@SECLEVEL=2

  4. Update SAP Profile Parameters:

    • Log in to your SAP system and navigate to the profile parameters.
    • Update the parameters related to TLS/SSL to enforce strong ciphers and protocols. For example:
      ssl/ciphersuites = 135:PFS:HIGH::EC_P256:EC_HIGH
ssl/client_ciphersuites = 150:PFS:HIGH::EC_P256:EC_HIGH
ssl/protocols = !SSLv2:!SSLv3:TLSv1.2:TLSv1.3

  5. Restart SAP Services: After making the necessary changes, restart the SAP services to apply the new configurations. You can do this using the following commands:

    sudo su - <sidadm>
stopsap
startsap

  6. Verify the Configuration: Use tools like openssl s_client or online SSL/TLS testing services to verify that the changes have been applied correctly and that the system is no longer vulnerable.

entry.title: Assistance Required for Enhancing ME5A Report
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

We are currently facing a requirement related to the ME5A report that needs your expert guidance. The details are as follows:

Requirement Overview:

  • We need the ME5A report, which typically shows purchase requisitions, to be updated such that if a purchase requisition exists for a material, the report should also display the header material (finished product).
  • The purchase requisitions should be shown as sales order-specific and collective.
  • In the MD04 transaction, when we open the order report, purchase requisitions are displayed, and at a higher level, subassemblies are shown with their corresponding finished products.
  • We need these finished product numbers to be included in the ME5A report.

Request for Guidance:

Please suggest how we can achieve this enhancement. Specifically:

  • Are there any relevant function modules (FMs) or BAPIs that can be used for this purpose?
  • Can you advise on the linkage between the necessary tables, such as EBAN (Purchase Requisition), MARA (Material Master), VBAK (Sales Order Header), and STPO (BOM Items)?

Your expertise on how to approach this task or any pointers to documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support and assistance

entry.title: Re: I am trying to get the value of data table using getv...
entry.content: Hi Bill_Froelich, I want to get the inputted value in a data table. Let's say my data table has two columns: Material Name: This column contains material names coming from a REST API. Quantity: This column is where I will enter the values of the quantity for each material. After clicking on the submit button, all the materials and their quantities should be posted through a POST API. This is my use case. I am following the tutorial about data tables in this blog: What's New in Mobile Development Kit Client 23.4 url of blog - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-mobile-development-kit-client-23-4/ba-p/13557735

entry.title: Re: How to consider the stock from Blocked/Quality in MRP...
entry.content:
Hi,

The Quality stock will be considered in the MRP as per the standard SAP. 

If you want to exclude the quality stock for a few meterials then it can be handdled by two ways.

The BAdI mentioned by an expert @DominikTylczyn. Other ways would be a sepearte storage location and exclude it from the MRP. In OMIR for the storage location to be set to 1 if you are in ECC and assigned to those materials . If you are using S4H then MRP area would be needed.

entry.title: SAP Security Patch Day – December 2024
entry.content:
On December 10, 2024, SAP released its monthly Security Patch Day updates, addressing several vulnerabilities across various products, including SAP NetWeaver AS, SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform, SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP Web Dispatcher, and more. Below, we provide an overview of the most critical vulnerabilities, their risk levels, and recommended actions to safeguard your SAP landscape.

Critical and High-Priority Vulnerabilities

  1. [CVE-2024-47578] Multiple Vulnerabilities in SAP NetWeaver AS for JAVA (Adobe Document Services)

    • CVSS Score: 9.1 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver AS for JAVA (Adobe Document Services)
    • Description: A set of vulnerabilities that can compromise sensitive information and allow significant impact on system integrity and availability.
    • Priority: HotNews

    This vulnerability requires immediate attention as attackers with administrative privileges (PR:H) can exploit it to access critical data and disrupt SAP systems.

  2. [CVE-2024-47590] Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in SAP Web Dispatcher

    • CVSS Score: 8.8 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP Web Dispatcher
    • Description: A high-severity XSS vulnerability that enables attackers to inject malicious code into the web interface, potentially leading to session hijacking, content spoofing, and more.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

    This vulnerability is exploitable without prior authentication (PR:N), emphasizing the urgency of applying the patch.

  3. [CVE-2024-47586] NULL Pointer Dereference in SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP and ABAP Platform

    • CVSS Score: 7.5 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP
    • Description: This vulnerability can lead to a denial of service (DoS), causing critical unavailability of the affected system.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

  4. [CVE-2024-54197] Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in SAP NetWeaver Administrator (System Overview)

    • CVSS Score: 7.2 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver Administrator
    • Description: SSRF vulnerabilities can allow unauthenticated attackers to access internal resources, potentially exposing sensitive data or leveraging trusted connections.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

  5. [CVE-2024-54198] Information Disclosure via RFC in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP

    • CVSS Score: 8.5 (AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP
    • Description: This high-risk vulnerability enables attackers with low privileges (PR:L) to exploit complex scenarios (AC:H) to access critical information or compromise system integrity.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

Medium- and Low-Priority Vulnerabilities

  1. [CVE-2024-47582] XML Entity Expansion in SAP NetWeaver AS JAVA

    • CVSS Score: 5.3
    • Description: A vulnerability in XML processing that could lead to system overload or potential denial of service.

  2. [CVE-2024-32732] Information Disclosure in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform

    • CVSS Score: 5.3
    • Description: An issue that could result in the leakage of non-critical but sensitive information.

  3. [CVE-2024-42375] Multiple Unrestricted File Upload Vulnerabilities in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform

    • CVSS Score: 4.3
    • Description: Allows attackers with limited privileges to upload unwanted files, potentially affecting data integrity.

  4. [CVE-2024-47585] Missing Authorization Check in SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP and ABAP Platform

    • CVSS Score: 4.3
    • Description: Insufficient authorization checks could allow unauthorized access to specific data.

  5. [CVE-2024-47577] Information Disclosure in SAP Commerce Cloud

    • CVSS Score: 2.7
    • Description: A low-severity information disclosure vulnerability requiring administrator privileges.

  6. [CVE-2024-47576] DLL Hijacking in SAP Product Lifecycle Costing

    • CVSS Score: 3.3
    • Description: A local vulnerability requiring user privileges. It is relevant for securing developer and administrator workstations.

Conclusion

The December SAP Security Patch Day highlights critical fixes addressing a wide range of vulnerabilities. Many of these require immediate action to prevent potential data breaches, system compromises, or denial-of-service attacks.

We strongly encourage analyzing these vulnerabilities promptly and applying the necessary patches. Taking a proactive approach will minimize risks and maintain the security of your SAP landscapes.

 

SAP Penetration Testing: https://redrays.io/sap-penetration-testing/

SAP Security Training: https://www.udemy.com/course/sap-security-core-concepts-and-security-administration/

entry.title: Re: ATP Check with Batch Determination
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm wondering why don't you use the batch determination at the sales order level.

You use batch determination for the sales order to find batches for set specifications. You can also reserve batches, requirements are transferred to material requirements planning (MRP) via sales orders.

In contrast to deliveries, batch splits are not possible for sales orders.

Determine Batches in Sales Orders | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: How to integrate HCM with other non SAP Apps?
entry.content:
Hey All, 
how we integrate SAP HCM to other Apps( non SAP). I have a product ( Testfuse) that conduct the candidate assessment. I want to integrate this into SAP HCM to help the requirement process. 

The scenario of integration will be as :

  • When candidate gets shortlisted the details of candidates ( email) should be sent to testfuse to invite a candidate for an assessment. 
  • The candidate will be then invited by testfuse and they perform the assessment in testfuse.
  • After the assessment the job fitness score will be calculated in testfuse and recruiter observe the results of candidate.
  • When  recruiter  change the status of the candidate in testfuse the status should also be changed in HCM. 


So what will be the guidelines to implement this integrations and also we have oauth to verify the integrated apps in testfuse. 

Anyone help to implement this integration. 

HCM (Human Capital Management) SAP Cloud Integration for data services

Any help on this will be very much appreciated.

Thanks,
Krishna.

Keerthana

entry.title: Resolving Corrupted ZIP Files in SAP CPI During Ariba ITK Integration
entry.content:
Issue: 
In SAP CPI, when retrieving procurement information from Ariba using ITK, large data volumes cause the ZIP file in the response to become corrupted. This corruption prevents the ZIP Splitter from extracting and processing the file.

Root Cause:
When the data volume in Ariba is large, Ariba sends null bytes in the response ZIP file to keep the connection alive. These null bytes precede the actual ZIP content, causing the file to appear corrupted.

Upon investigation, we noticed that a valid ZIP file should start with the header 50 4B (the standard ZIP signature). However, in the corrupted files, null bytes (00) were added before this header, rendering the file invalid.

Investigation Details:

  • Exported ZIP files from Ariba via ITK were inspected using a Hex Editor (e.g., Notepad++ with a HEX plugin).
  • The corrupted file had a significant number of null bytes (00) before the valid ZIP header (50 4B or similar).
  • These null bytes are intentional and part of Ariba's mechanism to keep the connection alive during long-running downloads.

Solution:
To resolve this issue, a Groovy script was developed to remove the null bytes from the ZIP file before processing. This script ensures the file integrity is restored by verifying and cleaning the ZIP header.

Here is the Groovy script:

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.util.zip.ZipInputStream
import java.util.zip.ZipOutputStream
import java.util.zip.ZipEntry
import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Get the ZIP file from the message body as a byte array
    byte[] zipBytes = message.getBody(byte[])

    // Remove null bytes only before the first occurrence of 0x50 0x4B
    byte[] cleanBytes = removeNullBytesBeforeSignature(zipBytes)

    // Repackage the cleaned bytes into a valid ZIP file containing all files
    byte[] reZippedBytes = repackageZip(cleanBytes)

    // Add the re-zipped file as an attachment in the message log (optional)
    def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
    if (messageLog != null) {
        messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("ReZipped_File", new String(reZippedBytes, "ISO-8859-1"), "application/zip")
    }

    // Return the re-zipped bytes as a new InputStream to the message body
    message.setBody(new ByteArrayInputStream(reZippedBytes))
    return message
}

// Function to remove null bytes only before the first occurrence of 0x50 0x4B
byte[] removeNullBytesBeforeSignature(byte[] fileBytes) {
    int indexOfPK = findSignatureIndex(fileBytes, [0x50, 0x4B] as byte[])
    if (indexOfPK == -1) {
        // If the signature is not found, return the original array
        return fileBytes
    }
    // Separate the data before and after the signature
    byte[] beforeSignature = fileBytes[0..<(indexOfPK)]
    byte[] afterSignature = fileBytes[indexOfPK..<fileBytes.length]

    // Remove all null bytes from the section before the signature
    beforeSignature = beforeSignature.findAll { it != 0x00 } as byte[]

    // Combine the cleaned part with the rest of the data
    byte[] result = new byte[beforeSignature.length + afterSignature.length]
    System.arraycopy(beforeSignature, 0, result, 0, beforeSignature.length)
    System.arraycopy(afterSignature, 0, result, beforeSignature.length, afterSignature.length)

    return result
}

// Function to find the index of the first occurrence of a byte signature
int findSignatureIndex(byte[] data, byte[] signature) {
    for (int i = 0; i <= data.length - signature.length; i++) {
        boolean match = true
        for (int j = 0; j < signature.length; j++) {
            if (data[i + j] != signature[j]) {
                match = false
                break
            }
        }
        if (match) {
            return i
        }
    }
    return -1
}

// Function to repackage cleaned bytes into a valid ZIP containing all original files
byte[] repackageZip(byte[] cleanedData) {
    ByteArrayOutputStream byteArrayOutputStream = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
    ZipOutputStream zipOutputStream = new ZipOutputStream(byteArrayOutputStream)

    // Read the cleaned bytes as a ZIP file
    ZipInputStream zipInputStream = new ZipInputStream(new ByteArrayInputStream(cleanedData))

    ZipEntry entry
    while ((entry = zipInputStream.getNextEntry()) != null) {
        // Copy each entry to the new ZIP file
        zipOutputStream.putNextEntry(new ZipEntry(entry.getName()))
        byte[] buffer = new byte[1024]
        int bytesRead
        while ((bytesRead = zipInputStream.read(buffer)) != -1) {
            zipOutputStream.write(buffer, 0, bytesRead)
        }
        zipOutputStream.closeEntry()
        zipInputStream.closeEntry()
    }

    zipInputStream.close()
    zipOutputStream.close()

    return byteArrayOutputStream.toByteArray()
}

entry.title: Forward Shipment Cost Items Incorrect
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are facing error in VI01 t code, where the freight charges in condition type is working properly, but the delivery tab is no coming and while selecting transfer error is arising "Forward Shipment Cost Items Incorrect".

Please help in this issue. 

Screenshots attached for your reference.

entry.title: Material Availability Check in Production and Planning Delivery Process Effect of EWM
entry.content:
Material Availability Check in Production

 

In Planing Delivery, customers indicate that they will collect materials from us on specific dates that we will have available stock.

If we try to prepare delivery before shipping dates, we encounter the following error in EWM.

Messeage no. VL198

Normally there can be many reasons for the error 'Item XX: Only 0 ST from material XXX are available,' but today we will focus on one of them.

 

Firstly let me tell about available stock in MRP

Before beginning production, it's important to ensure that all material components assigned to a process order are available by the required dates. The system only checks materials that are managed in inventory and have the item category "L" (stock components).

There are two ways to perform availability checks: automatically or manually.

  1. Automatic Check:
    • Availability can be checked automatically during the creation of a process order or when the order is released.
    • In the system’s customization settings, you can configure whether availability should be checked automatically, based on the order type and the plant.
  1. Manual Check:
    • You can always run an availability check manually if needed.

The scope of the availability check is defined by two factors:

  • The checking group (set in the material master record).
  • The checking rule (configured in process order settings).

Together, these factors determine:

  • Which MRP (Material Requirements Planning) elements are considered.
  • Which inventory categories are included.
  • Whether the replenishment lead time should be factored in.

In the customization settings for process orders, you can define the basis for the availability check. There are two main types of checks:

  1. Availability Check Against Planned Independent Requirements

In this method, the system checks the open planned independent requirement quantities created for each component. It does not consider ATP (Available-to-Promise) quantities, receipts, or stock levels.

This approach is ideal when:

  • Assembly planning or phantom assembly planning is used for components.
  • A quick estimate of material availability is needed, and precise results aren't necessary.

When using this method:

  • The system calculates the committed quantity for the components based on planned independent requirements.
  • This committed quantity is copied to the planned order header.
  • Unlike ATP logic, the committed quantity is not recorded in the dependent requirements.
  • Future availability checks can only commit quantities that haven’t already been consumed by previous checks.

This method does not calculate the following:

  • Total confirmation date
  • Partial confirmation date or quantity
  • ATP quantity
  1. Availability Check Based on ATP Logic

In this method, the system dynamically checks if the dependent requirements for each component can be fulfilled by current stock, receipts, or issue elements. Each time you run the check, the system recalculates the situation in real time.

If the required quantity can be fulfilled by the requirement date, the system:

  • Records this quantity as the committed quantity in the dependent requirements.
  • Reduces the ATP quantity by the committed quantity.

In subsequent availability checks, only the remaining ATP quantity is available to fulfill additional requirements.

 

Solution:

If you don’t need to follow of available quantity and to skip the error, you need to change the available stock check as 'No Check' from MM03 -> MRP3

But if you need to control of available stock check for planning delivery schedule process then you have to choose an available stock.

 

What is the effect of this button? (Active or inactive)

If availability check is active:
Current stock: 100
Reservations: 70
Incoming deliveries: 50
Net stock = 100 + 50 - 70 = 80

If availability check is not active:
Current stock: 100
Incoming deliveries: 50
Net stock = 100 + 50 = 150

Regards!

entry.title: Can I automatically create a project in SAP Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi 

I am trying to create a project automatically in Sap Public cloud.

For Example, if I give Project type as RD Projects and Project Profile, the project should be created Automatically. Is it Possible?

entry.title: Re: Hard stop configuration in Charm to prevent change owners approving their own changes.
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions.

entry.title: New SAP mobile service Client app for android v24.11.0 not working properly?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I recently updated my Mobile Service Client app to version 24.11.0. After the update, I've encountered a couple of issues with my MDK app:

  1. Alignment Issue: The page name is not showing in the action bar as expected. Instead, it appears below the action bar with some space between the action bar and the page name.
  2. Number Input Issue: I'm unable to input numbers into simple properties. Although the keyboard type is set to number, it does not accept the entered numbers.

Has anyone else experienced these issues? Any suggestions or solutions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

do you have an exception sub-process with the "error end" event configured ? This should handle the Post Processing action.

entry.title: Re: SAP Work Zone: Fiori app not visible in Content Manag...
entry.content:
Is it solved in the meanwhile? If not, can you check the tile configuration in the manifest.json of your app. Is it configured? It could be that it is not configured and because of that not shown.

Check the documentation for tile visualisation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/expose-html5-applications-in-sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition

sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM4_VAL=CHALLENGE_STATUS

sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM7_VAL=RISK_LOSS
sfuser.extendeduser.field.mapping.data.COL_NUM8_VAL=IMPACT_LOSS

@Harish_Vatsa 
 
SF-Dan

entry.title: Re: How to execute SI-CHECK when there are multiple produ...
entry.content: Hi Yuji, Running report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK with option "New relevance check in Online mode" is required on all productive clients. Just the consistency check should be run on the client 000. For CVI_COCKPIT related questions, please contact LO-MD-BP-SYN for further information. Thank you for your understanding. Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: I am trying to get the value of data table using getv...
entry.content: In that case you would use the getChanges() API call to get the values entered and then loop over them to post to the backend.

entry.title: Single Sign On SAP GUI
entry.content:
In SAP, we are using the service nwbc_sapguilink to enable SSO (Single Sign-On) for SAP GUI.

However, it seems that nwbc_sapguilink does not create a logon group when generating shortcuts.

For example, the shortcut is created with guiparm='/H/192.168.0.7/S/3200'.

How can we modify it so that the shortcut is generated using a logon group, like guiparm='/M/192.168.0.7/S/3600/G/GROUP'?

Regards, Arnold.

entry.title: Re: Unable to get Modal Dialog box in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Any update about this function?

entry.title: Re: New SAP mobile service Client app for android v24.11....
entry.content:
For the caption, in 24.11 we added support for the PreferLargeCaption setting as part of the action bar enhancements.  You can try setting this to false which should then display as you expect it.

For the number input I am not aware of this issue and will need to look into it further.  Can you share your simple property field metadata?

entry.title: Re: workflow orden mantenimiento
entry.content:
You should ensure the workflow for preventive maintenance orders doesn't skip the authorization step and verify, if the user creating the order has the authority to skip approval.
Also, see if the process for preventive maintenance orders needs approval. If not, adjust it.
Confirm if the workflow is activated when an order is created from a preventive plan. If not, adjust the settings.

entry.title: Re: CAP application with external API on BTP
entry.content:
You want to have a fiori elements app on top of your cap app that uses an external odata service and use the create and edit function if I understand it correctly?

For this, you need to enable draft on the entity which doesn’t work out of the box with cap for external api’s. The npm package that dinu proposes could help with that but I haven’t used it before. An alternative could be that you use a temporary table in between with draft enabled. In the after create event handler you can send the data to your external api.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation – SMTP Mail Destination using Microsoft 365 email provider
entry.content:
Hi @Anusha_N_Gowda ,

Apologies for the late response, have you already sorted out the issue? If not, could you please connect with me via email karthik.sathya.narayana.murthy@sap.com and I will see how I can help you.

entry.title: Re: Adding headers during Fiori Deployment
entry.content: As far as I’m aware this is not possible but in the end you always have a history in git. As long as you have that as a trace you can consider using a technical user for the deployment.

entry.title: Re: sap cap xs-security grant authorities for various app...
entry.content:
Ensure the app names in grant-as-authority-to-apps match the client IDs exactly, including case sensitivity.
Remove one entry to see if it works with only one app. If it does, the issue may lie in the second app’s configuration.
Use cf oauth-token to check if the app’s token includes the required scope and correct aud (audience).

If it still fails, may you should focus on the UAA service bindings and ensure each app's permissions are set up correctly.

entry.title: Re: How to Fetch Logged-In User Details Using SAP User AP...
entry.content:
Normally those fields are available in that api: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-portal-service/sap-cloud-portal-service-on-cloud-foundry/user-api-service

userid depends on how the authentication is configured. Name contains the subject name identifier and in most cases this is the userid but not always. You have some other options like reading the jwt token using cap or reading cis: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/show-current-user-details-in-cloud-foundry-apps-with-ias/ba-p/13432965

entry.title: Re: SCASE / UKM_CASE change/add function buttons into ALV...
entry.content: To add custom function buttons to the ALV overview in SCASE/UKM_CASE, you may can use the SE80 to locate the program or screen controlling the ALV grid and find the method managing the toolbar, like SET_TOOLBAR or similar. Use an enhancement point or BADI to add your button and implement the HANDLE_USER_COMMAND or equivalent method to process your button's functionality. Then activate the enhancement and verify the button appears and works without affecting standard functions. May that works.

Please Suggest.

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Cloud Public Edition: Unable to see the Italy...
entry.content: Hi Marina, thanks for your replay, Can you please help me to understand that the payment method field is mandatory in the e-invoice xml is by Italy government or its an SAP limitation?

entry.title: Re: How to create New unit of measure by using t-code CUN...
entry.content:
Hello @sandyshinde 

A new unit of measure is defined in the CUNI transaction. The configuration is straightforward and you can use an existing unit as a template. 

However, the conversion 1pcs=6000 DLI ( Die Life) should not be defined in CUNI. Conversions like 1kg = 1.000g or 1m = 10cm ca be defined only between units of measure in the same dimensions. PCS is dimensionless unit of measure. Therefore the conversion 1pcs=6000 DLI ( Die Life) needs to be defined in master data e.g. alternative units of measure of a material.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to read a SAP Note
entry.content: Yes, incognito mode solved the issue for me. The point is that the note is there and is available. The problem is more on a browser side.

entry.title: Re: Need Help Identifying the Best Processing Instruction...
entry.content:
Hello Juan Ignacio,

Please investigate if you can use interpretation algorithm 019 for processing the outgoing payments on the bank statement.
When you run the payment run, the system will post the payment in transit on a g/l account.
When you process the bank statement you can automatically clear the payment in transit when you use algorithm 019.

To achieve this you should know the external transaction code which is in the bank stement. Then you can assign the correct posting rule AND interpretation algorithm to it.

Best Regards,
Henk Hoogerbrug

entry.title: Re: Please share your experience - new API Management survey
entry.content:
Hi Bendelac, 

This is a great initiative from your side, it give us an opportunity to connect with the product team. 

I worked on Google APIGEE for the past three year, and working with SAP APIM was easier for me. I find lot of similarities in both of the product. But there are feature which Google has but SAP APIM doesn't such as SharedFlows (here we have Policy Templates but they help us to copy the policies but change in policy template cannot be replicated in existing proxies) , JSON payload validation - as SAP APIM offers Rest API, there should be a mechanism to validate the payload structure before passing the request to backend. 

Also, it would be great if product team add more content on help portal related to API Management available policies.

entry.title: Re: ATP Check with Batch Determination
entry.content: Hi Rupesh,

entry.title: Test Suite Dashboard - Custom Fields Implementation
entry.content:
Introduction

From SP13 Focused build test suite dashboard provides access to define custom fields by enhancing the configurations in Test suite dashboard Analytical List Page filters, Chart or Table fields. 

This Blog provides the detailed steps to configure custom fields for ALP pages. 

Pre-requisite

  • Understanding The New “Test Suite Dashboard”
  • Test Suite Dashboard – Overview Page
  • Test Suite Dashboard: Detail Pages

Test Suite Dashboard - Custom field implementation

This custom field can also be added as measures or dimension by dashboard users as additional filter or added to the chart or table.

Steps to add Custom property:

Go to SPRO -> SAP Solution Manager -> Focused Build -> Test Suite Extensions -> Test suite dashboard -> Define Custom Fields for Analytical list Pages OR

Each new custom field should be defined in properties. This will be mapped to relevant ALP based on selected view.

  • The view lists all the ALP for which custom fields can be defined.
  • The field name should be derived from the corresponding structure.
  • Add field label in property label
  • In type both characteristic or measure can be defined
  • Select the filter type for the custom field.

For example, custom properties Date Test is defined for defect status tile.

Property needs to be assigned to dimension of the details page in order to properly perform data collection along with the technical model of the Focused Build Test Suite Dashboard.

Define the dimension for corresponding property by selecting the property. 

Add dimension and corresponding Table and field name for custom field.

Table for which the custom field is added is entered along with the relevant parent table/ view. Edit or add the join condition. This logic is used to retrieve the value of custom fields.  

Save the changes and click on generate button to integrate the custom property into the Test Suite Dashboard model.

The custom fields defined can be selected in filter, chart and Table

BADI Enhancement for Custom Fields

Enhancement Spot: /SALM/TM_DASH_CUST_PROP_ENH
BAdI Definition: /SALM/TM_DASH_CUST_PROP_BADI
Interface: /SALM/IF_TM_DASH_CUST_PROP_ENH
Filter: CUSTOM_PROPERTY (Name of Custom Property in maintenance view /SALM/TM_DASHCPV)

In SE19 create implementation for Enhancement spot /SALM/TM_DASH_CUST_PROP_ENH

The enhancement provides access to user to modify the text values of custom property in Test suite dashboard, Multiple implementations can be created for the BADI definition /SALM/TM_DASH_CUST_PROP_BADI. By default, the text values are derived from domain values from custom property. Users can modify the values by implementing the GET_TEXT_VALUE_SET method.

Data type of custom text property within Test Suite Dashboard by default is 100 characters.
Data type can be changed by implementing the method SET_TEXT_PROPERTY_TYPE this enhancement spot and run the generation of custom properties in view /SALM/TM_DASHCPV (transaction SM34).
 

Please feel free to provide feedback either directly here in the comment section or in case of questions, you can submit them here. You are also encouraged to follow SAP Solution Manager tag here and Focused Build for SAP Solution Manager tag here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started (Part 1)
entry.content: @Martin_Kuma please note that another blog has been published, covering some of the aspects you have outlined, including e.g. exit variables in SAP Datasphere, please see this blog here: SAP Blogs | SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started (Part 2).

entry.title: Re: CER006
entry.content: Hello Anne, Certification is moved on the Learning page, but CER001 and CER006 curse will be there as per the mention the page. right no We are unable add course in the shopping bucket or we can't procced the payments for the CER001/006 course.

entry.title: Leveraging Dynamic Application Generation and GenAI for Real-Time Business Insights
entry.content:
Introduction

In the SAP ecosystem, most of us have interacted with business objects (like purchase order, maintenance order etc.) in one way or another. They are integral to any application created for clients or internal use, providing access to essential business data. However, the conventional process of connecting business objects to our applications, especially when performing specific actions on the data, can be tedious and time-consuming.

This blog post introduces an innovative architectural approach to dynamically generate application for real time business insights of business objects harnessing the GenAI capabilities like summarization in just a few simple steps, significantly reducing the effort and time.

Blog post series on Leveraging Dynamic Application Generation and GenAI for Real-Time Business Insights:

  1. Business Value & overview of the entire use-case/application (this blog)
  2. Comprehensive guide for setting up the application (TBD)
  3. Langchain Summarization Techniques & Leveraging GenerativeAI Hub via Custom Model (click here)

Problem Statement

To appreciate the significance of this blog, it's crucial to understand the current process of integrating business objects into applications and its time-consuming nature. Imagine you are a business owner using SAP to manage your operations, utilizing business objects to retrieve and derive insights from your data. With multiple departments like HR, Suppliers, and more, each with its own business object, the conventional approach requires creating separate extension applications for each business object.

Typically, this involves finding the APIs from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, creating a Domain, Service, and UI for each business object, and integrating it with SAP BTP services and SAP S/4HANA to access the data. Finally, you need to deploy it to the SAP Build Work Zone. Repeating this process for each business object increases computing resources, development time, and maintenance efforts significantly as shown below.

Explanation: If you have n number of business objects (BO1 to BOn) that need summarization of all the details, the entire setup and all its components must be repeated for each one. For each object, separate applications with Domain, Service, and UI components are developed and integrated into business systems along with BTP services by different teams. This process is repeated for each of the n objects, making it time-consuming, resource-intensive, and requiring increased maintenance efforts.

Solution

Now, imagine having a single application capable of displaying and performing actions on any number of business objects you need. Expand this vision to include the ability to achieve this with just one file, where you simply provide the details of the required business objects.

This blog post introduces a proof of concept (PoC) which enables you to gain insights by creating a single application that can display data for multiple applications that can cut across multiple master data business objects and summarize latent texts for various objects through a simple, one-configuration-file deployment.

The sample application source code includes a single configuration file where you can specify the details of relevant objects and indicate any dependencies between them if necessary. This file outlines object details such as object name, API url, destination name, search fields, list fields for the UI, and settings needed for the summarization process. By using this file, you can deploy and create app tiles in the SAP Build Work Zone, providing quick access to summarized information.

Upon deployment, the sample application dynamically generates the necessary artifacts, setting up the domain, service layers, and user interface to ensure flexibility and scalability for summarizing multiple business objects. It processes data from these business objects and transforms it into business insights using LangChain’s Summarization Chain techniques, such as Stuff, Map Reduce, and Refine, all connected to GenAI Hub through the CAP plugin “cap-llm-plugin.” Once deployed, app tiles can be configured to provide role-based access to different business users or personas.

This new approach simplifies the application development, integration by reducing the need for multiple extension applications and streamlining development and maintenance efforts, ultimately saving significant time and resources. By leveraging dynamic application generation and GenAI, it delivers efficient, real-time business insights.

This application has the capability to customize the summarization as per their need for any object via Standard Inputs and Open Ended options.

Standard InputOpen Ended

Standard Input option uses default prompt where the end user can configure the summary structure (paragraph or point based), word length and tone of voice (casual, neutral or formal) whereas Open Ended option uses self-defined prompts given by the end user based on which summarized content will be provided.

The standout feature of this application is its ability to dynamically provide business object details and summarized information for multiple objects using a single configuration file. Additionally, its powerful summarization capability allows you to select specific fields for summarization, making it ideal for managing extensive data that could otherwise hinder decision-making. This feature offers a concise overview, streamlining the data review process and enabling faster, more reliable business decisions with just one click.

Here is a video of the sample application: Demo Video

Architecture

In the above architecture, a CAP-based application accesses APIs for necessary business objects (either SAP solutions or third-party) via the SAP Destination Service, through its application logic layer. According to a configuration file provided by the end user, the application logic dynamically generates the domain, service, and UI components as required. The data retrieved via the Destination Service is then processed through a summarization chain, which utilizes techniques such as stuff, map-reduce, or refine, depending on the length of data and tokens required. This process involves creating a custom model to access SAP GenAI Hub via the cap-llm-plugin ( cap-llm plugin enables seamless integration with LLMs while anonymizing sensitive data via SAP HANA Cloud Data Anonymization, ensuring confidential information is not exposed to the LLM). The application is secured with XSUAA for authentication and integrated with SAP Build Work Zone. This integration ensures that all app tiles created for the business objects are accessed based on user’s role.

Scenario Set Up and Technical Details

To set up the scenario, certain prerequisites and initial configurations are required in SAP BTP and SAP S/4HANA. For detailed instructions, please refer to the following resources:

  • GitHub Repo: https://github.com/SAP-samples/real-time-business-insights-with-genai 
  • Scenario Set Up Guide: Refer to the part-2 of the blogpost (TBD)
  • Summarization Technical Details: Langchain Summarization Techniques: Leveraging Generative AI Hub via Custom Model

Note: Please note that this sample application has been created for proof of concept (PoC) purposes. Customers and partners are welcome to use the code, modify it, customize it to their specific needs, and maintain it for productive use.

Future Enhancements/ Roadmap

While our application already significantly streamlines data management and enhances decision-making processes, there is still potential for further improvements.

In Phase 2, we aim to add more value by introducing the following features:

  1. Simplified Business Object Configuration:
    We plan to replace the current configuration file setup with a user-friendly interface that allows users to select entities and fields directly. This enhancement will make the application accessible and easy to use for non-technical users, removing the need for manual configuration file editing.

  2. Human Feedback Loop for Summarization:
    To optimize the summarization response, we will implement a human feedback loop. During this phase, users will receive summarized responses based on different summarization techniques (stuff, map_reduce, refine). Users will be prompted to rate these summaries, helping us determine which technique delivers the best results. The feedback collected will enable us to set the most effective summarization method as the default.

  3. Support for REST APIs and Kyma Integration:
    We intend to expand our application's capabilities by including support for Business Objects accessed via REST APIs. Additionally, we will re-engineer the application setup to be compatible with Kyma, enhancing its flexibility and scalability.

These planned enhancements are designed to further simplify the user experience, improve the accuracy and relevance of data summaries, and broaden the application's compatibility with various APIs and platforms. By continuously evolving and refining our solution, we aim to deliver even greater value to our users, empowering them to make smarter, data-driven business decisions with ease.

You can watch the demo here that highlights our application's capabilities and showcases its seamless, efficient approach to transforming latent data into actionable insights, significantly enhancing the decision-making processes and business operations.

Conclusion

We hope this blog has given you a brief idea on how our application leverages the power of SAP GenAI Hub and advanced summarization techniques to transform vast amounts of business information into actionable insights. Our solution offers a seamless, efficient, and dynamic approach for various Business Object Summarization. With a single configuration file, businesses can automate the creation and maintenance of user interfaces, ensuring quick access to comprehensive data and its summaries across LOBs. This capability enhances decision-making while significantly reducing the time and effort traditionally required for such processes.

By adopting this solution, businesses can unlock the full potential of their data, driving smarter, more informed decisions and ultimately achieving greater success.

At last, I would like to extend my heartfelt gratitude to my colleagues whose insights, support, and collaboration have been invaluable throughout this PoC. So Special thanks to @Ajit_K_Panda and @Aryan_Raj_Sinha  for their valuable contributions in PoC development and @PVNPavanKumar and @anirban-sap for their leadership support.

entry.title: Re: ATP Check with Batch Determination
entry.content: Hi Rupesh - The issue with sales order batch determination is, as you indicated, the limitation of being able to use a batch split. So, if there is a high order value environment, doing a manual search would not be optimal. However, what we would need to do, it seems, is that we have a modification in ATP check where the system finds all the possible batches, cumulates the qty across all the batches and then reserves the stock for the sales order. The challenge, though, as usual is that without a batch in the sales order, As you suggested, a reservation would need an enhancement to create one for the order in question and then release the same during delivery processing which could be a challenge as well. Would appreciate inputs on how else we could achieve the desired results.

entry.title: Unattended Automation Failure in SAP Build Process Automation in Remote Virtual Machine
entry.content: I have been using screen automation (SAP Logon 800) along with action groups and email actions in my flow. The flow runs perfectly during attended automation. However, when I configure the flow with a scheduled trigger in unattended mode and disconnect from the virtual machine, the flow fails to recognize the SAP Logon screen. As a result, it sends a failure email. Even though I have an Unattended Automation License, what could be the issue?

entry.title: Re: Employee clocking in at 11:30PM for a midnight shift
entry.content:
Hi btoombs,

unfortunately there is no good workaround at the moment. We have this topic on our backlog list and I hope we can provide a decent solution for it in 2025 - but no comittments on this.

I don´t see any workaround to be honest. Logical day assignment and correct overtime calculation is quite complex and at least I don´t a way to overcome it via business rules or time valuations (else we would have already provided a solution / blog post on it).  Some customers have for example a combined daily and weekly overtime calculation and you cannot always take for granted that hours recorded before the shift are overtime, might be he leaves earlier that day or has some unpaid leaves during the week (in the case of weekly overtime calcuation).

The only thing I see is that you cut out those times before 00:00 and create an alert on it. And then an admin would need to create manual allowances to get the employee paid an overtime premium. I know, not ideal....

Sorry for not being able to give a more positive answer - we know the urgency of this topic and as mentioned, plan a solution for it. 

Best regards

Volker Ruof

entry.title: Re: Material Availability Check in Production and Planning Delivery Process Effect of EWM
entry.content: If you know all this... why is this a question?

entry.title: Enhancing Failure Analysis with AI-Powered Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! Today, I’m thrilled to share a project that bridges traditional failure analysis methods with modern AI capabilities. Leveraging the power of React and UI5 Web Components, I developed a Fiori app tailored for industrial maintenance workflows. Unlike standard forms for failure analysis, this application integrates artificial intelligence to provide actionable insights and recommendations for preventing recurring issues.

In this blog post, I’ll walk you through the journey of building this application, highlighting the technologies used, the architecture, and how it addresses real-world challenges in maintenance processes.

 

preview results:

Problem Statement
In the industrial environment, failure analysis is critical to reducing downtime and ensuring efficient operations. Traditional approaches involve:

  • Documenting failures using structured forms.
  • Creating action plans (e.g., 5W2H, 5 Whys).
  • Relying on human expertise to derive insights and solutions.

While effective, these methods can be time-consuming and dependent on the analyst’s experience. This led me to explore how AI could enhance the process by:

  • Automating the generation of recommendations.
  • Standardizing responses to common problems.
  • Providing data-driven insights to augment human decision-making.

Application Overview

The app combines the familiar functionality of SAP Fiori forms with advanced AI capabilities. Here are its key features:

Core Functionalities

  1. Failure Documentation

    • Users can input detailed descriptions of failures.

    • Form fields are structured around methodologies like 5W2H.

  2. AI-Powered Suggestions

    • Integrated with an AI model (via API) that analyzes the problem.

    • Provides suggestions to prevent similar issues in the future.

  3. Action Plan Generation

    • Automatically drafts a preliminary action plan based on AI insights.

  4. History and Trends

    • Allows users to review past analyses and identify recurring patterns.

Technology Stack

Frontend

  • React: For building a dynamic and user-friendly UI.

  • UI5 Web Components: To ensure consistency with SAP Fiori design principles.

Backend

  • Node.js: For handling API requests and routing.

  • Python (Flask): Hosting the AI model and processing user inputs.

AI Integration

  • The AI model leverages natural language processing (NLP) to:

    • Understand problem descriptions.

    • Suggest actionable recommendations.

 

Development Highlights

Why React and UI5 Components?

Combining React with UI5 components allowed me to:

  • Leverage modern frontend capabilities while maintaining SAP’s design consistency.

  • Benefit from React’s flexibility and UI5’s pre-built, accessible components.

there is a very good demonstration:

https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/build-your-own-react-application-with-ui5-web-components-for-react/ev-p/283244

 

AI Model Integration

  • Trained on historical failure reports and action plans.

  • Continuously improves with feedback from users.

Overcoming Challenges

  1. Ensuring SAP Fiori Design Compliance:

    • Adapted UI5 Web Components for a native look and feel.

  2. Optimizing AI Recommendations:

    • Incorporated feedback loops to refine suggestions.

Demonstration

Here’s how the app works:

  1. Input the Problem:

    • The user describes the issue in free text and selects related categories.

  2. Generate Suggestions:

    • The AI analyzes the description and presents actionable recommendations.

  3. Review and Finalize:

    • Users review AI-generated insights and finalize the action plan.

  4. Save and Track:

    • The data is stored for future reference and trend analysis.

Fiori App

Ai solution 

 

Follow bellow one sample used in fiori solution:

 

follow the suggestion from model:

' .. 

  • Establish a comprehensive preventive maintenance schedule:
    • Define the ideal lubrication frequency for bearings based on manufacturer recommendations and operating conditions.
    • Include regular motor temperature checks and vibration measurements.
  • Continuously monitor motor temperature:
    • Install temperature sensors on bearings and other critical points.
    • Set up temperature alerts to notify the maintenance team of potential issues.
  • Train the maintenance team:
    • Provide comprehensive training on motor lubrication requirements and best maintenance practices.
    • Establish clear procedures for lubrication and monitoring.
  • Use high-quality lubricants:
    • Select lubricants appropriate for operating conditions and motor requirements.
    • Maintain a stock of lubricants and ensure their proper application.
  • Regularly analyze maintenance records:
    • Track lubrication, motor temperature, and vibration data to identify trends and predict potential problems.
    • Adjust the maintenance schedule as needed.
  • Promote a proactive mainte.nance culture:
    • Emphasize the importance of regular preventive maintenance and continuous monitoring.
    • Recognize and reward the maintenance team for excellence in maintenance and failure prevention.' 

Benefits and Impact

This application aims to:

  • Save Time: Automate the recommendation process.

  • Enhance Decision-Making: Provide data-driven insights.

  • Standardize Processes: Reduce variability in failure analysis.

Next Steps

This project is just the beginning! I plan to:

  • Enhance AI Capabilities: Improve suggestion accuracy with more data.

  • Integrate with SAP: Connect directly to SAP PM for seamless workflows.

Conclusion

Building this application has been a rewarding experience, combining SAP’s robust ecosystem with cutting-edge AI technologies. I’m excited to hear your feedback and suggestions for improvement.

Feel free to connect with me here on the SAP Community or via LinkedIn. Let’s collaborate to make failure analysis smarter and more efficient!

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/bernardo-caldas/

entry.title: Re: bin determination according to FEFO rule
entry.content:
@Tomas_Norkus 

"And what about if we have to pick 20 pc and we have expir date in two bins? In my case system determined a bin with oldes stock and stock if stock quatity match. In this case ewm not follow FEFO."

What is the question? Sorry, don't really understand what the system does.

Brgds

Juergen

I see now that CVI_COCKPIT is a separate issue. I apologize for my misunderstanding.
Thank you for your clarification.🤝
Best regards,
Yuji Tazawa

entry.title: Re: Different number appears instead of user name in Mess...
entry.content: Any update on tis

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Meetup Istanbul - December 6th, 2024
entry.content:
In Friday’s session, we discussed several key topics. We focused on planning CodeJam activities and strategies to increase engagement and awareness for Functional Inside Track were explored. Additionally, we emphasized the importance of organizing events to boost community awareness and encouraging SAP Community members to take active roles.

Regular Zoom sessions for knowledge sharing, voting for sponsorship venues, and ensuring diverse participation in events were also proposed. Our guiding principle remains the “Pay it Forward” approach. ⭐️

entry.title: Re: Reduction of PIRs by sales orders
entry.content: i not able to find the SAP note 1715491

entry.title: Re: Implementation of Local Extensions for SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (ICVCP3_EN_Col01)
entry.content:
Not able to find or access the course on SAP Learning Hub?

Please create a ticket to get help: https://support.learning.sap.com/ -> Contact Us.

entry.title: Re: bin determination according to FEFO rule
entry.content: Hello, here is my scenario: I have two storage bins in the same storage type, each containing handling units (HUs) with different expiration dates: BIN1: 01/10, 01/11, 01/12 BIN2: 01/09, 01/13, 01/14 I need to pick a quantity of 20 with the earliest expiration dates. This means the required quantity of 20 should come from the stock with expiration dates 01/09 (from BIN2) and 01/10 (from BIN1). However, EWM is creating one warehouse task for the full quantity of 20 from BIN2, based on the earliest expiration date. Question what is the reason? I'm expecting to have two warehouse tasks

entry.title: SAP SEGW OData project with CDS Reference - Chance to extend Metadata without own SEGW Service?
entry.content:
Hi,

we have extended the SAP Standard CDS View C_SalesOrderWl_F1873 to add a existing field with value help annotations. 

Now we have the problem that SAP has used the SEGW to expose this CDS views as an service. 

Is there a option to extend the metadata of the segw service without a customer project? Because if we create a new service we would ne also an adaption project in the app to exchance the service.

Problem: New Value Help is deteced but I cant modify SAP Standard Project.

Thx & Bye

entry.title: Re: ATP Check with Batch Determination
entry.content: That is one of the options, however the drawback is that only one batch can be specified on sales order item. Therefore a customer requirement has to be fulfilled from a single batch only.

entry.title: CAP using subqueries for case separation
entry.content:
Hi,

From the following table I am trying to get the the records grouped by RefA and RefB columns with the maximum value of Value1. In case the Value1 is same for multiple records, then the maximum value of Value2.

So only in this example the records 1 and 5.

IDRefARefBValue1Value2
1a1b1510
2a1b1315
3a1b135
4a2b1425
5a2b1435

Ideally I would like to do this operation with only one query and no server-side post processing.

In SQL the following query gets me the correct result.

-- Option 1: Subqueries for SQLite
SELECT
    a.RefA,
    a.RefB,
    MAX(a.Value1) AS max1,
    MAX(
        CASE
            WHEN a.Value1 = subquery.max1
	    THEN a.Value2
            ELSE NULL
        END
    ) AS max2
FROM table1 as a
JOIN (
    SELECT RefA, RefB, MAX(Value1) AS max1
    FROM table1
    GROUP BY RefA, RefB
) AS subquery
    ON a.RefA = subquery.RefA AND a.RefB = subquery.RefB
GROUP BY a.RefA, a.RefB;

-- Option 2: Window function in case separation (general)
SELECT
	RefA,
	RefB,
	MAX(Value1) AS max1,
	MAX(
		CASE
			WHEN Value1 = MAX(Value1) OVER(
				PARTITION BY RefA, RefB
			)
			THEN Value2
			ELSE null
		END
	) AS max2
FROM table1
GROUP BY RefA, RefB;

However, I don't know what the correct approach for this is in CAP. Can you help me with this?

Thanks in advance.

Atakan
